name: St. Francis River
difficultyRating: Class II-IV (upper whitewater); calmer below Sam A. Baker SP
description: Missouri's only whitewater river. The upper St. Francis through Millstream Gardens / Silver Mines (the Tiemann Shut-ins) is a 2.3-mile, rain-dependent Class II–IV pool-drop run that spikes and drops fast (USFS; American Whitewater #2921). Below the whitewater it mellows and floats more like a normal stream toward Sam A. Baker State Park and Wappapello Lake.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (Millstream Gardens CA) and USDA Forest Service, Mark Twain NF (Silver Mines Rec Area); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Roselle, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (USGS page 403'd; site # from search snippet). The American Whitewater run (#2921) keys its runnable range to 'the Roselle gauge'. DATA-CONTINUITY RISK: USGS dropped this gauge from the national network once (funding); confirm it is currently live and that the app can poll it before relying on it. USACE MVS St. Louis District (SF_tab) is a possible fallback feed.

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Patterson, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (USGS page 403'd; co-located with NWS PAZM7). Long-record gauge; discharge(00060)+gage height(00065); USACE St. Louis District coop. Represents the lower river toward Wappapello.

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Saco, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (search-surfaced run 2). Mid gauge between Roselle and Patterson; confirm active + which reach it best represents.

gauges.name: St. Francis River at Wappapello, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (search-surfaced run 2). Lowest gauge, at Wappapello Lake — likely below the floated reaches; verify before use.

sections.name: Upper Whitewater (Millstream Gardens to Silver Mines / Tiemann Shut-ins)
sections.description: The reason to add this river: a 2.3-mile Class II–IV rain-fed pool-drop run through the Tiemann Shut-ins. Only whitewater on a Missouri river. Runnable mainly in spring high water; spikes and drops fast; committing at high flow. The condition badge here is a paddler runnable-range, not a lazy-float curve.

representativeGauge.rationale: American Whitewater #2921 quotes the runnable range against the Roselle gauge; MWA keys race go/no-go to a St. Francis level display
representativeGauge.knownBias: gauge site # unverified; historical funding-driven outages

thresholds.source: American Whitewater #2921 / St. Louis Outdoor Adventures (via WebSearch synthesis, 2026-07): 'The Roselle gauge generally has to be above 3 feet for the river to be at its best.' Paddler D-bridge conversion: when gauge <6.0 ft, (gauge − 3) × 15 = D-bridge level in inches.

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: Below the runnable minimum the shut-ins are unrunnable rock (drag/portage). NUMERIC too_low anchor UNKNOWN — capture AW min from #2921.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rain-driven flash rises turn Class II–IV into dangerous high water fast; pushy hydraulics in the shut-ins. High end can be lethal. NUMERIC high/dangerous anchors UNKNOWN — capture AW max + NWS ROZM7 flood stages.
characteristics.dropRateNote: Drops fast after a rise (pool-drop, small drainage) — capture specifics

localKnowledge: Only river in Missouri classified as whitewater; kayaked during spring high water (USFS Silver Mines).
Pool-drop character: swift stretches separated by calm pools (USFS).
Missouri Whitewater Championships (MWA) run this reach each spring; race is contingent on variable water levels — 'verify before traveling'.

sections.name: Lower Float (Silver Mines toward Sam A. Baker SP / Wappapello)
sections.description: Below the shut-ins the gradient eases; the river becomes a calmer float, notably slower below Sam A. Baker State Park (MCFA). Floated more like a normal Ozark stream. Candidate for a per-section river_type divergence from the whitewater default.

takeOut.name: Sam A. Baker State Park area / lower accesses

representativeGauge.rationale: Patterson gauge (co-located with NWS PAZM7) is the lower-river reference

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN — capture; low water mainly affects speed on this reach
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rises arrive from upstream rain; calmer than the shut-ins but still rain-responsive.

localKnowledge: MCFA notes the water is 'slower below' Sam A. Baker State Park (mile 66.5).

accessPoints.name: Millstream Gardens Conservation Area
accessPoints.notes: Whitewater put-in. S of Hwy 72 in Madison County, ~11 mi W of Fredericktown / ~11 mi E of Ironton. MCFA mile 20.2.

accessPoints.name: Silver Mines Recreation Area (Hwy D)
accessPoints.notes: Whitewater take-out / lower put-in. MCFA mile 23.2. USFS campground + day-use (loop count unverified).

accessPoints.name: Sam A. Baker State Park access
accessPoints.notes: MCFA mile 66.5; water slower below here.
